project: AntiScam — Gen Digital AI-First Internship Assignment, Option B
author: Aleksandr Kross <krossale@fel.cvut.cz>

interactions.prompt_summary: Set up a minimal Android Jetpack Compose skeleton for a scam message detector. MVVM, package com.krossale.antiscam, AGP 8.5.2, Kotlin 2.0.21, Compose BOM 2024.12.01. Create all Gradle files, AndroidManifest, and MVVM layer stubs.
ai_output: settings.gradle.kts, build.gradle.kts (root + app)
gradle/libs.versions.toml (version catalog)
gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties
AndroidManifest.xml
Kotlin stubs: RiskLevel, ScamAnalysisResult, ScamAnalyzer, ScamDetectorUiState, ScamDetectorViewModel, ScamDetectorScreen, MainActivity
interactions.developer_changes: None — accepted as-is. AGP 8.5.2 + Gradle 8.7 pairing already validated.
interactions.outcome: Full buildable project skeleton in place

interactions.prompt_summary: Implement ScamAnalyzer.kt with local keyword and regex-based heuristic analysis. Classify as Safe/Suspicious/Dangerous. Include dangerous keywords (password, bank account, gift card, bitcoin...), suspicious keywords (click here, act now, limited time...), and URL pattern detection (IP-based URLs, shortened URLs, suspicious TLDs). Return confidence 0–100 and plain-language explanation.
ai_output: dangerousKeywords list (15 terms)
suspiciousKeywords list (13 terms)
suspiciousUrlPatterns: IP regex, shortener regex (bit.ly/tinyurl/goo.gl/t.co), TLD regex (.xyz/.top/.click/.link)
analyze() scoring: dangerous = min(97, 70 + hits*8 + urlCount*6); suspicious = min(85, 50 + hits*6 + urlCount*12)
buildExplanation() producing natural-language output
developer_changes: Capped confidence at 97 (not 100) to imply no heuristic is perfect
Removed overly broad suspicious keywords ('your account', 'congratulations', 'selected', 'confirm') that caused false positives on safe messages
Replaced with more specific phrases: 'confirm your account', 'you have been selected', 'unusual sign-in'
Changed 'limited time' → 'limited time offer' to reduce false positives
Refactored two private methods buildDangerousExplanation/buildSuspiciousExplanation (incorrectly took List<Regex>) into single buildExplanation(keywords, urlCount, dangerous) taking correct types
interactions.outcome: ScamAnalyzer.kt — production-quality heuristic engine with 7 passing unit tests

interactions.prompt_summary: Create ScamDetectorScreen.kt using Material3. Include: dark navy header card, OutlinedTextField for input, LazyRow of example chips, full-width Analyze button, ResultCard with risk badge + LinearProgressIndicator + explanation text. Use theme colors: Navy, Blue, SafeGreen, SuspiciousAmber, DangerousRed.
ai_output: ScamDetectorScreen (StateFlow collector) + ScamDetectorContent (stateless layout)
AppHeader — dark navy Card with AntiScam branding
InputSection — OutlinedTextField with placeholder
ExampleMessagesSection — LazyRow of OutlinedButton chips
ResultCard — risk badge (Surface + color), LinearProgressIndicator, HorizontalDivider, explanation text, colored BorderStroke
Color.kt with full palette
Theme.kt wrapping MaterialTheme with lightColorScheme
developer_changes: Rejected deprecated Divider, replaced with HorizontalDivider (Material3 1.3+)
Updated LinearProgressIndicator to lambda form progress = { score / 100f } (non-deprecated API)
Removed isAnalyzing: Boolean from UiState — unnecessary since local analysis is synchronous (instant)
Rejected collectAsStateWithLifecycle suggestion — requires extra dependency, collectAsState() sufficient for this prototype
interactions.outcome: ScamDetectorScreen.kt — clean Material3 Compose UI with all Material3 APIs used correctly

interactions.prompt_summary: Add an in-memory analysis log that persists across analyses within a session. Show history entries with timestamp, input snippet, risk level, and confidence. Allow the user to hide/show and clear the log.
ai_output: AnalysisLogEntry data class (id, inputSnippet, result)
ScamDetectorUiState extended with history: List<AnalysisLogEntry>
onAnalyzeClicked() extended to prepend entry (reverse-chronological order)
onClearHistory() method on ViewModel
HistorySection composable with AnimatedVisibility (show/hide toggle) and Clear button
HistoryEntryRow with colored risk dot, snippet text, 'Safe · 93% · 14:32:01' subtitle
interactions.developer_changes: Kept it in-memory (no Room persistence) — matches assignment scope and keeps code simple to explain in demo video.
interactions.outcome: Full session-scoped analysis log, visually integrated into UI

interactions.prompt_summary: Generate unit tests for ScamAnalyzer and ScamDetectorViewModel covering: safe → Safe, phishing → Dangerous, suspicious phrase → Suspicious, empty → Safe+50, multi-keyword confidence range, example selection, blank analyze does nothing, analyze produces result, input change clears result.
ai_output: ScamAnalyzerTest.kt — 5 AI-generated tests
ScamDetectorViewModelTest.kt — 8 AI-generated tests
developer_changes: Added 2 tests to ScamAnalyzerTest: 'safe message has non-empty explanation', 'prize scam with bit.ly URL → Dangerous'
Added 2 tests to ScamDetectorViewModelTest: 'multiple analyses build history in reverse order', 'clear history empties the log'
Marked all AI-generated tests with comment: // AI-generated test — reviewed and refined by developer
interactions.outcome: 17 passing unit tests total. ./gradlew test → BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 6s

interactions.prompt_summary: Convert anti.svg to Android VectorDrawable XML. SVG has drop-shadow filter (unsupported), dark rounded rect background (rx=10), and a shield/antenna path. Produce arc-based path data for rounded rect and preserve original logo path.
ai_output: anti_logo.xml VectorDrawable
Logo path fill=#3266AA stroke=#E2DDD0
ic_launcher_foreground.xml and ic_launcher_background.xml for adaptive icon
mipmap-anydpi-v26/ launcher XMLs
developer_changes: Drop-shadow filter correctly identified as unsupported — removed
Manually verified arc path coordinates against original SVG geometry
Moved anti.svg to app/src/main/assets/ (Android rejects .svg in res/drawable/)
interactions.outcome: Custom adaptive launcher icon displayed correctly on device

interactions.prompt_summary: Fix build errors: Java 25 incompatibility with Gradle 8.7, missing gradle-wrapper.jar, compileSdk mismatch.

errors_encountered.cause: System default JDK was Java 25; Gradle 8.7 supports max Java 21
errors_encountered.fix: Created ~/.gradle/gradle.properties with org.gradle.java.home=/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64; changed Android Studio Gradle JVM to embedded JBR-21

errors_encountered.cause: Only android-36 SDK installed on this machine
errors_encountered.fix: Changed compileSdk and targetSdk from 35 to 36 in app/build.gradle.kts

errors_encountered.cause: Binary not committed in original repo
errors_encountered.fix: Generated using Gradle 8.7 from ~/.gradle/wrapper/dists/ cache: JAVA_HOME=.../java-21 $GRADLE_BIN wrapper --gradle-version=8.7

errors_encountered.error: app/build/ not in .gitignore, staged build artifacts on first commit
errors_encountered.fix: Added app/build to .gitignore before first commit

interactions.outcome: Clean build. App installed on Samsung Galaxy A12 via ./gradlew installDebug

interactions.prompt_summary: Initial planning prompt: given an empty repo and the assignment PDF, asked AI to recommend folder/project structure, describe MVVM architecture, define layer responsibilities, list recommended Kotlin classes/files, describe UI→ViewModel→Domain→Result data flow, define test structure, and suggest simplifications suitable for a small internship project.
ai_output: Recommended 3-layer MVVM layout: ui/ (Screen + ViewModel + UiState), domain/ (Analyzer + Result + RiskLevel), data/ (ExampleMessages)
Described StateFlow-based data flow: user input → ViewModel.onAnalyzeClicked() → ScamAnalyzer.analyze() → ScamAnalysisResult → UiState update → Compose recomposition
Identified exact Kotlin files needed (7 source files + 2 test files)
Recommended against Repository/Room/Hilt for internship scope
Suggested keeping ViewModel analysis synchronous to simplify unit testing (no coroutines needed)
developer_changes: Added AnalysisLogEntry.kt and history log feature not mentioned in initial plan
Kept synchronous analysis as suggested — confirmed correct decision during test phase
interactions.outcome: Clear project blueprint established before writing any code

interactions.prompt_summary: Generate 5–6 realistic email/SMS examples covering different risk levels (Safe ~10–15%, Suspicious ~35–60%, Dangerous ~75–97%) and scenario types: bank alerts, account verification, prize scams, subscription notifications, credential requests. Each example should include subject, body, expected risk level, estimated confidence, and explanation.
ai_output: Safe example: legitimate StreamView subscription renewal reminder — no urgency, no links, no credential requests
Suspicious example: account sign-in alert with bit.ly link and 24-hour confirmation window
Dangerous example: prize winner scam with tinyurl and 15-minute expiry
Dangerous example: bank security alert requesting account verification with suspicious domain
Dangerous example: password reset phishing with fake Microsoft branding
Each example annotated with expected RiskLevel and confidence range
developer_changes: Used the Safe and Dangerous examples as the built-in ExampleMessages.kt chips (Bank Alert Scam, Prize Winner Scam, Safe Message)
Suspicious example used as manual test input during device testing
Rejected the Microsoft password reset example from ExampleMessages — too long for a chip label
interactions.outcome: 3 built-in example messages integrated into the app; additional examples used for manual QA

interactions.prompt_summary: Asked AI to read the assignment PDF, inspect the full project (source, tests, README, screenshots, Git status), check what was completed vs missing, verify all Option B requirements, assess whether newer May 19 screenshots should replace old ones, run tests, estimate rubric score, and provide a prioritized fix checklist.

Estimated score: ~82/100 without video, ~88–90/100 with video
Prioritized checklist: (1) record video, (2) update screenshots, (3) add terminal screenshots to README
developer_changes: Replaced all README screenshots with May 19 versions showing full explanation text and Analysis Log
Added Suspicious result screenshot (was missing from README)
Added Claude terminal screenshots to README section 11 (AI Interaction Log)
Added ai_interaction_log.json to project root with hyperlink in README

interactions.prompt_summary: Review generated code for correctness and best practices.
recommendations_accepted: Remove isAnalyzing: Boolean from UiState (synchronous analysis needs no loading state)
Use lambda form for LinearProgressIndicator progress parameter

recommendations_rejected.reason: Requires lifecycle-runtime-compose dependency; collectAsState() is sufficient for this prototype scope

recommendations_rejected.recommendation: Use Hilt for dependency injection of ScamAnalyzer
recommendations_rejected.reason: Assignment scope does not require DI; simple direct instantiation is easier to explain during demo video
